commands.review_summary:
The PR stages top-level buffer-only `message.send` attachments into outbound media and wires the staged path through `media`/`mediaUrl`/`mediaUrls` before channel delivery.

PR surface: Source +113, Tests +118. Total +231 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current `message.send` builds deliverable media from media/path/URL hints while ordinary send skips attachment hydration, so a top-level buffer without an explicit media path never becomes a media URL.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs a contributor or maintainer update for both the media-cap blocker and external real behavior proof; repair-only automation should not proceed while the proof gate is mock-only.

target.title: fix(outbound): materialize buffer-only message.send attachments
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `message.send` could fail when callers supplied attachment bytes only via `buffer` + `filename` + `contentType`, because the durable send path expected `media`/`mediaUrl` paths and never materialized buffer-only payloads.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue [#90768](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/90768): in-memory artifact delivery workflows hit a dead end unless callers manually stage a local file first (`impact:message-loss`).

What is the intended outcome?

- Buffer-only `send` params are staged into the OpenClaw outbound media store and exposed as `media`/`mediaUrl`/`mediaUrls` before normal channel delivery, preserving the existing URL/path contract.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No per-channel buffer contracts, LM Studio/plugin changes, or `sendAttachment` behavior changes; explicit `media`/`mediaUrl`/`path` sources are left untouched.

What does success look like?

- `message.send` with only `buffer` + metadata delivers through `executeSendAction` with a staged outbound media path; buffer-only params without explicit media sources no longer fail the send assembly path.

What should reviewers focus on?

- `materializeSendBufferMediaParams` in `message-action-params.ts`
- `resolveOutboundAttachmentFromBuffer` in `media/outbound-attachment.ts`
- Regression tests in `message-action-params.test.ts` and `message-action-runner.media.test.ts`

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90768

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

- ClawSweeper review on #90768 (`clawsweeper:queueable-fix`, `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`, `clawsweeper:source-repro`)
- Narrower than related outbound media issues `#20258` / `#50312` per reporter

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Community report; ClawSweeper identified source-repro path and acceptance tests.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Buffer-only `message.send` attachments now materialize to outbound media paths before channel dispatch.
- **Real environment tested:** Local checkout on branch `fix/issue-90768-send-buffer-materialize`, Vitest on Windows host.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/outbound-attachment.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts -t "materializ|buffer-only send"`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts -t "materializes buffer-only send"`
- **Evidence after fix:**

```json
{
  "test": "materializes buffer-only send attachments into outbound media paths",
  "input": {
    "buffer": "YXJ0aWZhY3QgYnl0ZXM=",
    "filename": "artifact.txt",
    "contentType": "text/plain"
  },
  "observed": {
    "sendResult.mediaUrl": "<staged outbound path>",
    "fileContents": "artifact bytes",
    "executeSendAction.mediaUrls": ["<same staged path>"]
  },
  "vitest": "4 passed (outbound-attachment + params + runner media)"
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Staged file at `sendResult.mediaUrl` contains `artifact bytes`; `executeSendAction` receives the same path in `mediaUrl` and `mediaUrls`.
- **What was not tested:** Live channel delivery on reporter's self-hosted runtime; all channel plugins.
- **Proof limitations:** Deterministic Vitest harness with mocked `executeSendAction`; proves staging + send wiring, not end-to-end Telegram/Slack delivery.
- **Before evidence (optional):** `hydrateAttachmentParamsForAction` skipped `send`; `buildSendPayloadParts` only merged explicit media hints, so buffer-only sends lacked `mediaUrl` and failed `send requires text or media` or delivered without attachment.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/outbound-attachment.test.ts` → 2 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts -t "materializ|buffer-only send"` → 3 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts -t "materializes buffer-only send"` → 1 passed

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `resolveOutboundAttachmentFromBuffer` stages outbound buffers with filename/content type.
- `materializeSendBufferMediaParams` writes buffer-only send args to outbound media paths; skips when explicit media source exists.
- `runMessageAction` send integration verifies `executeSendAction` receives staged `mediaUrl`/`mediaUrls`.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Buffer-only `message.send` never converted `buffer` into a deliverable `mediaUrl` for the core send path.

If no test was added, why not?

- N/A — regression tests added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Staging buffer-only sends into the outbound media store when no explicit media path/URL was provided.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Skips materialization when any explicit media source or structured attachment URL/path is already present; reuses existing `saveMediaBuffer` outbound subdir and attachment max-byte limits.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer review

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI on fork PR

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Implements ClawSweeper-suggested buffer-to-temp-media normalization before channel dispatch on #90768.
target.author: liuwqgit
